meaning: n.<诗><古>情人,求爱者,乡村青年,乡村情郎
examples: At the very end of the poem, the uncouth swain, "rose, and twitch his mantle blue, tomorrow to fresh woods and pastures new."
The sun that was just dropped into the west or the uncouth swain?
This is the first moment, right now, "thus sang the uncouth swain."
The King's Speech won seven Baftas and Mr Swain's work was recognised in the acceptance speeches.
Bonham-Carter has previously worked with Mr Swain on projects including Sweeney Todd and Alice in Wonderland.
